session_note: S83 — Voltkin full audit + real-animation upgrade (user-queued S82, PDR v2 approved this session: 'Approved! work creatively, technically, pedantically, and thoroughly'). Full-tier batch PDR presented in-chat after A.0 audit (checkerboard baked into all 6 sprites 0% alpha + intro mp4; no key on sprite path; gray checkers survive 0.88 luma key; belly #FFEB6B luma .887 threshold collision). Council R1+R2 (Grok 4.20 reasoning + Gemini 2.5-pro) — Battle Ledger 7 rows, Grok mesh-warp alternative rejected on merits, single-atlas + global-tick-loop + dHash gate adopted from Grok, VFX library deferred per user, audio cues rejected-already-wired. User quality bar added in v2: MapleStory-grade aliveness (weight, anticipation, follow-through, personality); reject clips that read as improved frame-flipping. Model: Fable 5 (user-sanctioned S82 precedent).
council_result: CONVERGED (R2 synthesis: P2 probe upgraded to adversarial walk-cycle case; P3 adopted 2.75 vs 1.0 with Grok atlas optimization folded in; P4 unanimous with median-contamination validation; D5 VFX SPLIT resolved by user = defer)

priorities.label: S83 P1 — TRUE-ALPHA SPRITES: offline checkerboard matte of the 6 Voltkin frames (edge-connected flood fill + LAB tolerance + edge decontamination)
priorities.objective: scripts/matte-voltkin-frames.py converts the 6 shipped frames to true-alpha PNGs by removing the baked-in transparency checkerboard. Edge-connected flood fill (checker tones only), LAB-tolerance match, 2px edge decontamination. Originals in assets-source/ untouched.
priorities.testing: pixel probes: 0 border-connected checker pixels; >0% transparent; interior whites (eyes/teeth) intact; dHash character-region vs original ~unchanged; in-game live preview probe
priorities.check_method: S83 P1 (commit 24648a8): scripts/matte-voltkin-frames.py — measured checker model (tones 240/254, chroma<=5, ~56px cells) -> candidate mask chroma<=22 & luma>=224; scipy 8-conn labeling, border-connected components = bg (enclosed whites structurally safe behind black outlines); speck absorption <=48px near-achromatic islands inside bg; distance-transform feather 2px with per-pixel unmix against NEAREST actual bg pixel color (local gray-vs-white cell tone — Grok R1 refinement); transparent px RGB zeroed (premultiply-friendly). GATES: --verify 30/30 probes green across 6 frames (no checker-tone px in border ring [burst-frame chromatic rays at border correctly exempted after empirical probe: charge ring px cyan chroma 48-191, zap yellow chroma 39-185 = legitimate artwork]; every checker px transparent; 49-72% transparent; interior >=2px byte-identical np.array_equal; enclosed whites survive). VISUAL: all 6 dark-composite inspections clean — eyes/teeth/victory-stars/cyan-fists/motion-lines preserved, white starburst intact. Sizes 134-279KB (was 186-379KB). Byte-exact originals in assets-source/godly-voltkin/sprites/pre-s83-checkerboard/. Zero TS changes. In-game render probe deliberately deferred to P5 sweep (renderer changes again in P3; logged, not dropped).

priorities.label: S83 P2 — ADVERSARIAL VEO PROBE: walk-cycle clip seeded with cleaned idle frame; quality-bar gate for P3
priorities.objective: veo_generate image-to-video walk cycle on solid background. Gate: dHash drift, loop closure, motion variance (not pose-flipping), qualitative aliveness checklist at 128px render size. Contact sheet + GIF posted in-chat. Fail => ship P1+P4 only + carry-forward.
priorities.scope: assets-source/godly-voltkin/anim-probe/ (new working dir), no src/ changes
priorities.testing: dHash drift gate; first/last frame loop delta; frame-to-frame motion variance; visual checklist review
priorities.check_method: S83 P2 (commit 3215589): veo-3.1-generate-preview image-to-video, seed = P1-cleaned idle-1 flattened onto pure white (transparent seed would have flattened to black and merged with the outlines), 4s 720p 24fps, $0.50. Prompt = locked character spec verbatim + animation-principle language (weight shift, squash/stretch, follow-through, anticipation, 'mischievous gremlin energy') + 'walks IN PLACE like a video game sprite' + 'flat solid pure white background, no shadow, static locked camera'. GATE METRICS (gate.py): dHash drift vs seed 0-18/64 across sampled frames (<=24 bar, frame 1 = exact 0); mid-clip loop frames 29..37 closure 7.91 < 8.43 consecutive-frame baseline (near-seamless, no ping-pong needed); motion mean-abs-diff 35.5 with 0/47 near-static transitions (continuous real motion, NOT pose-flipping); centroid x-spread 41px (in-place); char area ratio 1.15 (size-stable). MATTE-ON-VIDEO validated: P1 algorithm + pillarbox handling on h264 frames -> 80.4% transparent, 0.54% feather band, dark-composite strips clean (loop-strip-matted.png), crown sparks chromatic-protected. Aliveness checklist @128px game scale: weight shift / bob / arm swing + follow-through / live expression / readable silhouette ALL present. VERDICT: PASS -> P3 proceeds. Contact sheet + GIF + matted strip posted in-chat per PDR. Probe mp4 retained as PRODUCTION walk clip (reuse, $0 extra).

priorities.label: S83 P3 — REAL ANIMATION (gated on P2): per-state Veo clips -> 12-15fps frames -> matte -> single atlas + manifest -> deterministic tick mapping
priorities.objective: Clips: walk/idle loop (chibi), charge windup, zap (lion), hurt, victory — Transformation Arc preserved. Atlas <=2048^2 + JSON manifest in public/godly/voltkin/anim/. voltkinFrames mapping extended: loops keyed on world.tick modulo, one-shots on ticksInState (zap lands on FIRE_TICK=30). Per-state fallback to cleaned static frame. Quality bar: MapleStory-grade aliveness; reject smoother-frame-flip clips. Code delta <=4KiB JS.
priorities.testing: vitest mapping determinism (loop modulo + FIRE_TICK sync + fallback); tsc; bundle delta check; live preview at game scale
priorities.check_method: S83 P3 (commit 4e3e257): 5 new Veo clips ($2.50; running total $3.00) — idle/charge on white, zap/hurt/victory on MAGENTA (achromatic-white decorations [dizzy swirls, victory stars, ray cores] survive a chromatic key; A.0-derived choice); despill pass min(R,B)>G -> G:=min(R,B) neutralizes h264 magenta smear (locked palette has no native pink = provably safe); scripts/build-voltkin-atlas.py packs 56 cells 256px into ONE 2048x1792 atlas quantized 256 colors (3184->540KB) + manifest; cross-clip scale normalized to each static frame's char-height fraction (state-to-state size continuity). voltkinFrames.currentAnimCell pure mapping: loops on world.tick, one-shots on ticksInState, zap apex==FIRE_TICK, form-swap boundaries identical to legacy so flashIntensity untouched. Renderer: manifest-last readiness latch, sticky-fail -> legacy 6-frame fallback retained, 512/cell scale comp, SEEKING bob retired on anim path; NEW velocity estimator (lastSeenPos + 15-tick hold) drives walk/idle AND facing — DISCOVERY: wire prevPos rehydrates = pos on the client mirror so S36 P6 facing NEVER worked on the 1v1 client; estimator fixes it render-side, no wire change. GATES: tsc clean; FULL vitest 1247/1247 (+10 incl. form-swap parity walk + production-manifest drift guard w/ no-cell-overlap + apex bounds + despawn-window fit); bundle 544.8KiB (+2.7KiB <=4KiB budget); LIVE preview real-host-loop: walk cells cycling exact 5-tick cadence, scaleX=-0.5 nativeFacing mirror proof, ATTACKING frame-exact t15 charge0+66ffff flash / t29 charge9 / t30 ZAP APEX 1024,768 / t44 zap5 / t45 idle, REAL BOND SEVERED underneath (LOCKED 13.15 mechanics executing untouched), white-px probe 1.54% (no card), walk->idle hold transition observed, zero console errors/warns. Screenshot posted in-chat.

priorities.label: S83 P4 — INTRO VIDEO FIX: temporal-median plate-difference matte -> composite onto black -> re-encode; lumaKey disabled
priorities.objective: Offline: median background plate (validated against explosion segment), per-frame diff matte, composite onto black, re-encode H.264 same budget. recipe lumaKey.enabled=false (kills belly-hole defect, removes runtime shader use). Fallback: Veo regen seeded with cleaned tv-glowing on solid black (~$0.35).
priorities.testing: frame-extract pixel probes pre/post; live preview: cinematic plays seamlessly over black, no squares, no belly holes
priorities.check_method: S83 P4 (commit 662def5): scripts/matte-voltkin-intro.py — temporal-median plate from 48 frames; checker-like candidate (achromatic & lum>=170) refined by 13px morphological OPENING (Grok median-contamination concern resolved EMPIRICALLY: the plate had baked-in STATIC lightning-arc cores; opening kills <=10px streaks, keeps ~45px checker cells; mask-vis inspected twice in-chat) + guarded 5px re-dilation; per-frame soft plate-difference key INSIDE mask only (static checker -> black, moving explosion/shards/creature kept); libx264 CRF 26 + AAC copy + faststart, 1345KB. VERIFY: first metric FALSE-FAILED at 6.82% counting the legitimate white explosion flash kept over the ring — probe-the-failure (P1 lesson) -> corrected metric counts NEAR-PLATE checker-tone survivors only: 0.06% worst. Visual 4-frame inspection posted in-chat (ring black, explosion intact, end creature+TV clean). recipe lumaKey.enabled=false -> plain-DOM video path (pre-S22), shader retired at runtime; belly-hole threshold collision eliminated by construction. In-page Chromium decode smoke via preview_eval: 6/6 former-ring probes (0,0,0). tsc clean; godlyRecipes 22/22. Source mp4 pristine in assets-source/ (rollback).

priorities.label: S83 P5 — VERIFICATION SWEEP: determinism tests, fog e2e, full e2e lane, bundle delta, live pixel probes, CI green
priorities.objective: Full gates: vitest suite green incl. new mapping tests; fog.spec 6-children intact; FULL local e2e lane; bundle <=550KiB charter with <=4KiB JS delta; live-preview probes (no checker survivors around creature; video seamless; animation feel at game scale); CI green on tip; LOCKED_DECISIONS render-note if needed.

verification.needle: ## Session 83 — Voltkin full audit + real-animation upgrade [COMPLETED 2026-06-10]

priorities.check_method: S83 P5 (commit 2b3a4b5): FULL local e2e lane 37 pass/1 skip 3.7m (fog 6-children z-order + reconnect + smoke + lobby + hazards all green); FULL vitest 1247/1247; tsc clean; bundle 544.9KiB (+2.8KiB session total <=4KiB budget, <550 charter). Adversarial CHECK Triumvirate: GEMINI-AUDITOR PASS 5/5/5/5; GROK-ANALYST 5 findings triaged inline vs ACTUAL diff -> 4 REJECTED with evidence (latch race = hallucinated structure, cells built before manifest-last assignment in one sync block; despill-cyan = math no-op, min(50,205)<205; FIRE_TICK literal = code imports VOLTKIN_* constants; DOM-video stacking = unchanged pre-S22 path, speculative), 1 ACCEPTED downgraded HIGH->LOW and SHIPPED as hardening (teleport >200px/frame ignored by estimator; future-dated lastMoveTick dropped on clock rewind); Gemini unison observation SHIPPED (per-creature loop phase offset id*7, serialized id keeps host/client agreement). RALPH:PATROL spec-conformance: all PDR contracts honored; one nuance logged — runtime fallback is whole-atlas (per-state rejection happened at clip-review time, all 6 passed); LOCKED_DECISIONS needs NO amendment (no luma/render rows in 13.15; all constants untouched). BACKLOG S83 entry written; PDR archived COMPLETED. CI on tip 2b3a4b5: E2E lane + Deploy both SUCCESS (verified before handoff).

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #measure-the-defect-before-modeling-it #verify-expectations-can-be-wrong: the 'white squares' bug was not a keying bug but a DATA bug — the generator drew a literal picture of a transparency checkerboard (0% real alpha even in RGBA files; the integration notes' 'PNG/RGBA' claim was true of the MODE and false of the CONTENT). The matte needed no AI: measured checker tones (240/254, chroma<=5) vs lightest body yellow (chroma ~148) gave a clean achromatic+bright+border-connected separation, and per-pixel unmix against the NEAREST bg pixel beat any global tone. Second lesson: when a verify probe fails, probe the FAILURE before loosening — charge/zap 'border ring opaque' px turned out chroma 39-191 = legitimate burst rays reaching the edge; the check's expectation was wrong, not the matte. Fix the assertion to forbid checker-tone survivors specifically, never blanket-relax.

P2 #probe-the-hard-case-not-the-easy-case #seed-prep-matters: Grok's challenge ('an idle clip proves nothing') was right about WHICH probe to run, wrong about whether to probe — the synthesized adversarial walk-cycle probe (motion + loop closure + in-place constraint, the three things most likely to fail) converted the whole P3 bet into ground truth for $0.50, and the probe clip itself became the production walk asset. Two craft details that made it work: flatten the transparent seed onto the TARGET background color before image-to-video (alpha flattens to black otherwise, merging with the outlines), and prompt with animation-PRINCIPLE vocabulary (weight shift, squash/stretch, follow-through, anticipation) + the locked character spec verbatim — the model honors craft language better than 'make it look good'.

P3 #background-color-is-a-matte-decision #live-probe-found-the-fsm-guarding-itself: choosing each Veo clip's background from the matte's FAILURE MODES (magenta for clips with achromatic-white decorations, white otherwise) plus a despill pass justified by the LOCKED palette ('no native pink' makes min(R,B)>G->lift-G provably safe) beat any single-background pipeline. Mid-priority A.0 discovery: serialized prevPos rehydrates equal to pos on the client mirror, so the S36 facing flip NEVER worked in 1v1 — render-side velocity estimation (own lastSeenPos + hold window) fixed walk/idle selection AND the legacy bug with zero wire change. And when the live ATTACKING probe came back 'wrong' (walk cells), the probe was actually catching the FSM bouncing a hand-injected ATTACKING state with no target bond — give the creature a REAL bond and the locked mechanics sever it under the new render: the best proof the swap is render-only.

P4 #median-plate-plus-morphology #verify-metrics-need-the-same-rigor-as-code: the temporal-median plate captured MORE than the checkerboard — static lightning-arc cores persisted across >half the frames and landed in the plate; keying them would have hollowed the arcs. A 13px morphological opening separated the two BY SHAPE (45px checker blocks vs <=10px streaks) — when tone can't discriminate, structure can. Second: the first --verify metric false-failed at 6.82% by counting the white explosion flash legitimately kept over the ring; the corrected metric (near-plate AND checker-tone) read 0.06%. A verification metric is code too — when it fails, autopsy the FAILING PIXELS before trusting either the metric or the pipeline.

P5 #check-triage-earns-its-keep #reviewers-without-the-diff-hallucinate: GROK-ANALYST CHECK returned FAIL with a CRITICAL 'readiness latch race' citing files and fields that don't exist (VoltkinRenderer.ts, manifest.cells[].rect) plus a claimed repro he cannot have run — reviewers fed a SUMMARY instead of the diff attack the summary's prose, not the code. The inline Opus triage (S154 protocol) rejected 4/5 findings WITH EVIDENCE (assignment-order proof, despill arithmetic, constant imports, unchanged legacy path) and still extracted real value: the 5th finding (estimator state across snapshot restore) was a genuine edge worth 12 lines of hardening, and Gemini's single observation (unison loops) shipped as a 1-line aliveness win. LESSON: an external FAIL verdict is a list of CLAIMS to verify, not a stop sign — but never skip the triage, because rejecting 80% still leaves the 20% that improves the ship.
session_status: S83 CLOSED — 5/5 priorities shipped (24648a8 true-alpha sprites, 3215589 walk probe PASS, 4e3e257 real animation atlas, 662def5 intro video black-composite, 2b3a4b5 sweep + CHECK hardening). ROOT CAUSE fixed everywhere: generator-baked transparency checkerboard (sprites 0% alpha + intro mp4). vitest 1247/1247; FULL e2e 37 pass/1 skip; bundle 544.9KiB<550 (+2.8KiB); generative spend $3.00/$10; protocol v7 + LOCKED 13.15 untouched (render-only, live-proven via bond-sever under new render). CHECK: Gemini PASS 5/5/5/5; Grok FAIL triaged -> 4/5 rejected with evidence, 1 hardened. NEXT: playtest round 5 incl. Voltkin feel on live site.
